title: Chat System — Message Channels
description: Create a ChatChannel class with name, members (set), and history (collections.deque with maxlen=100). Methods: join(player_name), leave(player_name), send(player_name, message) which appends a formatted entry to history, and get_history(count) which returns the last N messages. Create a ChannelManager with default channels 'global', 'trade', and 'help'. Methods: get_channel(name), create_channel(name), list_channels(). Support /channel style commands.
test_code:
# Tests for Step 5: Chat System
from collections import deque

assert 'ChatChannel' in dir(), 'ChatChannel class must be defined'
assert 'ChannelManager' in dir(), 'ChannelManager class must be defined'

# Test ChatChannel constructor
ch = ChatChannel('global')
assert ch.name == 'global', 'ChatChannel should store name'
assert isinstance(ch.members, set), 'members should be a set'
assert len(ch.members) == 0, 'members should start empty'
assert isinstance(ch.history, deque), 'history should be a deque'
assert ch.history.maxlen == 100, 'history maxlen should be 100'

# Test join/leave
ch.join('Alice')
assert 'Alice' in ch.members, 'Alice should be a member after join'
assert ch.member_count == 1, 'Should have 1 member'
ch.join('Bob')
assert ch.member_count == 2, 'Should have 2 members'
ch.leave('Alice')
assert 'Alice' not in ch.members, 'Alice should not be a member after leave'
assert ch.member_count == 1, 'Should have 1 member after leave'
ch.leave('Nobody')  # Should not error

# Test send
ch.send('Bob', 'Hello world!')
assert len(ch.history) == 1, 'Should have 1 message in history'
assert '[global] Bob: Hello world!' == ch.history[0], f'Message format wrong: {ch.history[0]}'

# Test send multiple messages
ch.send('Bob', 'Second message')
ch.send('Bob', 'Third message')
assert len(ch.history) == 3, 'Should have 3 messages'

# Test get_history
history = ch.get_history(2)
assert isinstance(history, list), 'get_history should return a list'
assert len(history) == 2, f'Should return 2 messages, got {len(history)}'
assert history[-1] == '[global] Bob: Third message', f'Last message wrong: {history[-1]}'

# Test get_history with more than available
history_all = ch.get_history(100)
assert len(history_all) == 3, 'Should return all 3 messages when asking for 100'

# Test deque max size
small_ch = ChatChannel('test')
for i in range(150):
    small_ch.send('Bot', f'Message {i}')
assert len(small_ch.history) == 100, 'History should cap at 100 messages'

# Test ChannelManager
mgr = ChannelManager()

# Test default channels exist
assert mgr.get_channel('global') is not None, 'global channel should exist'
assert mgr.get_channel('trade') is not None, 'trade channel should exist'
assert mgr.get_channel('help') is not None, 'help channel should exist'
assert isinstance(mgr.get_channel('global'), ChatChannel), 'get_channel should return a ChatChannel'

# Test list_channels
channels = mgr.list_channels()
assert isinstance(channels, list), 'list_channels should return a list'
assert sorted(channels) == ['global', 'help', 'trade'], f'Expected default channels, got {channels}'

# Test create_channel
new_ch = mgr.create_channel('party')
assert isinstance(new_ch, ChatChannel), 'create_channel should return a ChatChannel'
assert new_ch.name == 'party', 'Created channel should have correct name'
assert mgr.get_channel('party') is new_ch, 'Should be able to get the new channel'
assert 'party' in mgr.list_channels(), 'party should appear in list_channels'

# Test get nonexistent channel
assert mgr.get_channel('nonexistent') is None, 'Should return None for unknown channel'
acceptance_criteria: ChatChannel stores name, members set, and history deque with maxlen=100
join() and leave() manage the members set
send() formats messages as '[channel] player: message' and appends to history
get_history(count) returns the last N messages as a list
ChannelManager creates default channels: global, trade, help
ChannelManager.create_channel() creates and stores new channels
hints: Use collections.deque(maxlen=100) for automatic size limiting.
In get_history, convert deque to list and slice: list(self.history)[-count:]
In ChannelManager.__init__, loop through DEFAULT_CHANNELS and call self.create_channel(name).
Format chat messages with f'[{self.name}] {player_name}: {message}' for consistency.
